events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "in-progress", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 40, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 1.0, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": ["install-WRB", "install-flashing", "seal-laps"], "workable": [], "findings": ["Reverse-lapped WRB: upper layer is behind lower layer instead of overlapping it shingle-style", "Creates a direct path for water to enter wall assembly at horizontal and vertical laps", "Violates shingle-lap sequencing required for drainage plane"], "actions": ["Remove or release the affected WRB and flashing in the reverse-lapped area", "Reinstall WRB from bottom up so upper courses overlap lower courses", "Install flashing with upper WRB lapping over flashing and lower WRB under flashing", "Verify all laps are sealed per manufacturer instructions before continuing"], "rationale": "A WRB must be lapped shingle-style so each higher layer drains over the lower layer. Reverse lapping channels water behind the WRB, defeating the drainage plane and risking concealed moisture damage. The work is actively in progress and non-compliant, so it must be corrected before proceeding.", "references": ["IRC R703.1.1 / R703.2 - weather-resistant covering and flashing requirements", "IRC R703.4 / R703.7 - water-resistive barrier installation and drainage", "Manufacturer WRB installation instructions - shingle-lap sequencing and flashing integration"]}
